heading: Discretionary and targeted grants
label: 629g Discretionary and targeted grants
notes: References in TextThe Public Health Service Act, referred to in subsec. (f)(2)(A)(ii), is , . children.heading: Limitations on authorization of appropriations
children.label: a Limitations on authorization of appropriations
children.text: In addition to any amount appropriated pursuant to , there are authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section $200,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2025 through 2029.section 629f of this title

children.heading: Evaluation, research, training, and technical assistance
children.label: 1 Evaluation, research, training, and technical assistance
children.text: The Secretary shall reserve 3.3 percent for expenditure by the Secretary for the activities described in .section 629e of this title

children.heading: State court improvements
children.label: 2 State court improvements
children.text: The Secretary shall reserve 3.3 percent for grants under .section 629h of this title

children.heading: Indian tribes or tribal consortia
children.label: 3 Indian tribes or tribal consortia
children.text: The Secretary shall reserve 3 percent for allotment to Indian tribes or tribal consortia in accordance with subsection (c)(1).

children.heading: Improving the interstate placement of children
children.label: 4 Improving the interstate placement of children
children.text: The Secretary shall reserve $5,000,000 of the amount made available for fiscal year 2018 for grants under subsection (g), and the amount so reserved shall remain available through fiscal year 2022.

children.heading: Preventive services evaluation partnerships
children.label: 5 Preventive services evaluation partnerships
children.text: The Secretary shall reserve $5,000,000 for grants under for each of fiscal years 2026 through 2029.section 629e(f) of this title

children.heading: Kinship navigators
children.label: 6 Kinship navigators
children.text: The Secretary shall reserve $10,000,000 for grants under for each of fiscal years 2026 through 2029.section 627 of this title

children.heading: Indian tribes or tribal consortia
children.label: 1 Indian tribes or tribal consortia
children.text: From the amount (if any) reserved pursuant to subsection (b)(3) for any fiscal year, the Secretary shall allot to each Indian tribe with a plan approved under this subpart an amount that bears the same ratio to such reserved amount as the number of children in the Indian tribe bears to the total number of children in all Indian tribes with State plans so approved, as determined by the Secretary on the basis of the most current and reliable information available to the Secretary. If a consortium of Indian tribes applies and is approved for a grant under this section, the Secretary shall allot to the consortium an amount equal to the sum of the allotments determined for each Indian tribe that is part of the consortium.

children.heading: Territories
children.label: 2 Territories
children.text: From the amount (if any) appropriated pursuant to subsection (a) for any fiscal year that remains after applying subection (b) for the fiscal year, the Secretary shall allot to each of the jurisdictions of Puerto Rico, Guam, the Virgin Islands, the Northern Mariana Islands, and American Samoa an amount determined in the same manner as the allotment to each of such jurisdictions is determined under .1section 623 of this titleSo in original. Probably should be “subsection”.1

children.heading: Other States
children.label: 3 Other States
children.text: From the amount (if any) appropriated pursuant to subsection (a) for any fiscal year that remains after applying subsection (b) and paragraph (2) of this subsection for the fiscal year, the Secretary shall allot to each State (other than an Indian tribe) which is not specified in paragraph (2) of this subsection an amount equal to such remaining amount multiplied by the supplemental nutrition assistance program benefits percentage (as defined in ) of the State for the fiscal year.section 629c(c)(2) of this title
75 percent of the total expenditures by the State for activities under the plan during the fiscal year or the immediately succeeding fiscal year; or
the allotment of the State under subsection (c) for the fiscal year.

children.heading: Applicability of certain rules
children.label: e Applicability of certain rules
children.text: The rules of subsections (b) and (c) of shall apply in like manner to the amounts made available pursuant to subsection (a).section 629d of this title

children.heading: Targeted grants to implement IV–E prevention services, and improve the well-being of, and improve permanency outcomes for, children and families affected by heroin, opioids, and other substance abuse
children.label: f Targeted grants to implement IV–E prevention services, and improve the well-being of, and improve permanency outcomes for, children and families affected by heroin, opioids, and other substance abuse

children.heading: Purpose
children.label: 1 Purpose
children.text: The purpose of this subsection is to authorize the Secretary to make competitive grants to regional partnerships to provide, through interagency collaboration and integration of programs and services, services and activities that are designed to increase the well-being of, improve permanency outcomes for, and enhance the safety of children who are in an out-of-home placement or are at risk of being placed in an out-of-home placement as a result of a parent’s or caretaker’s substance abuse, and expand the scope of the evidence-based services that may be approved by the clearinghouse established under .section 676(d) of this title

children.heading: Mandatory partners for all partnership grants
children.label: A Mandatory partners for all partnership grants
i
children.text: The State child welfare agency that is responsible for the administration of the State plan under this part and part E.

children.label: ii
children.text: The State agency responsible for administering the substance abuse prevention and treatment block grant provided under subpart II of part B of title XIX of the Public Health Service Act [ et seq.].42 U.S.C. 300x–21

children.label: iii
children.text: The most appropriate administrative office of the juvenile court or State court overseeing court proceedings involving families who come to the attention of the court due to child abuse or neglect.

children.label: i
children.text: An Indian tribe or tribal consortium.

children.label: ii
children.text: Nonprofit child welfare service providers.

children.label: iii
children.text: For-profit child welfare service providers.

children.label: iv
children.text: Community health service providers, including substance abuse treatment providers.

children.label: v
children.text: Community mental health providers.

children.label: vi
children.text: Local law enforcement agencies.

children.label: vii
children.text: School personnel.

children.label: viii
children.text: Tribal child welfare agencies (or a consortia of the agencies).

children.label: ix
children.text: State or local agencies that administer Federal health care, housing, family support, or other related programs.

children.label: x
children.text: Any other providers, agencies, personnel, officials, or entities that are related to the provision of child and family services under a State plan approved under this subpart.

children.heading: Exception for regional partnerships where the lead applicant is an Indian tribe or tribal consortia
children.label: C Exception for regional partnerships where the lead applicant is an Indian tribe or tribal consortia
i
children.text: may (but is not required to) include the State child welfare agency as a partner in the collaborative agreement; and

children.label: ii
children.text: may not enter into a collaborative agreement only with tribal child welfare agencies (or a consortium of the agencies).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall award grants under this subsection, from the amounts reserved for each of fiscal years 2025 through 2029 under , to regional partnerships that satisfy the requirements of this subsection, in amounts that are not less than $250,000 and not more than $1,000,000 per grant per fiscal year.section 629f(b)(4) of this title

children.heading: Required minimum period of approval; planning
children.label: B Required minimum period of approval; planning

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: A grant shall be awarded under this subsection for a period of not less than 2, and not more than 5, fiscal years, subject to clauses (ii) and (iii).

children.heading: Extension of grant
children.label: ii Extension of grant
children.text: On application of the grantee, the Secretary may extend for not more than 2 fiscal years the period for which a grant is awarded under this subsection.

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: A grant awarded under this subsection shall be disbursed in two phases: a planning phase (not to exceed 2 years) and an implementation phase. The total disbursement to a grantee for the planning phase may not exceed the total anticipated funding for the implementation phase.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: II Exception
children.text: The Secretary, on a case-by-case basis, may waive the planning phase for a partnership that demonstrates that the partnership has engaged in sufficient planning before submitting an application for a grant under this subsection.

children.heading: Multiple grants allowed
children.label: C Multiple grants allowed
children.text: This subsection shall not be interpreted to prevent a grantee from applying for, or being awarded, separate grants under this subsection.

children.heading: Limitation on payment for a fiscal year
children.label: D Limitation on payment for a fiscal year
children.text: No payment shall be made under subparagraph (A) or (C) for a fiscal year until the Secretary determines that the eligible partnership has made sufficient progress in meeting the goals of the grant and that the members of the eligible partnership are coordinating to a reasonable degree with the other members of the eligible partnership.

children.label: A
children.text: Recent evidence demonstrating that substance abuse has had a substantial impact on the number of out-of-home placements for children, or the number of children who are at risk of being placed in an out-of-home placement, in the partnership region.

children.label: i
children.text: enhance the well-being of children, parents, and families receiving services or taking part in activities conducted with funds provided under the grant;

children.label: ii
children.text: lead to safe, permanent caregiving relationships for the children;

children.label: iii
children.text: improve the substance abuse treatment outcomes for parents including retention in treatment and successful completion of treatment;

children.label: iv
children.text: facilitate the implementation, delivery, and effectiveness of prevention services and programs under ; andsection 671(e) of this title

children.label: v
children.text: decrease the number of out-of-home placements for children, increase reunification rates for children who have been placed in out-of-home care, or decrease the number of children who are at risk of being placed in an out-of-home placement, in the partnership region.

children.label: C
children.text: A description of the joint activities to be funded in whole or in part with the funds provided under the grant, including the sequencing of the activities proposed to be conducted under the funding period for the grant.

children.label: D
children.text: A description of the strategies for integrating programs and services determined to be appropriate for the child and the child’s family.

children.label: E
children.text: A description of a plan for sustaining the services provided by or activities funded under the grant after the conclusion of the grant period, including through the use of prevention services and programs under and other funds provided to the State for child welfare and substance abuse prevention and treatment services.section 671(e) of this title

children.label: F
children.text: Additional information needed by the Secretary to determine that the proposed activities and implementation will be consistent with research or evaluations showing which practices and approaches are most effective.

children.label: A
children.text: Family-based comprehensive long-term substance use disorder treatment including medication assisted treatment and in-home substance abuse disorder treatment and recovery services.

children.label: B
children.text: Early intervention and preventative services.

children.label: C
children.text: Children and family counseling.

children.label: D
children.text: Mental health services.

children.label: E
children.text: Parenting skills training.

children.label: F
children.text: Replication of successful models for providing family-based comprehensive long-term substance abuse treatment services.

children.label: i
children.text: 85 percent for the first and second fiscal years for which the grant is awarded to a recipient;

children.label: ii
children.text: 80 percent for the third and fourth such fiscal years;

children.label: iii
children.text: 75 percent for the fifth such fiscal year;

children.label: iv
children.text: 70 percent for the sixth such fiscal year; and

children.label: v
children.text: 65 percent for the seventh such fiscal year.

children.heading: Non-Federal share
children.label: B Non-Federal share
children.text: The non-Federal share of the cost of services provided or activities conducted under a grant awarded under this subsection may be in cash or in kind. In determining the amount of the non-Federal share, the Secretary may attribute fair market value to goods, services, and facilities contributed from non-Federal sources.

children.label: A
children.text: demonstrate that substance abuse by parents or caretakers has had a substantial impact on the number of out-of-home placements for children, or the number of children who are at risk of being placed in an out-of-home placement, in the partnership region;

children.label: B
children.text: have limited resources for addressing the needs of children affected by such abuse;

children.label: C
children.text: have a lack of capacity for, or access to, comprehensive family treatment services;

children.label: D
children.text: demonstrate a track record of successful collaboration among child welfare, substance abuse disorder treatment and mental health agencies;

children.label: E
children.text: demonstrate a plan for sustaining the services provided by or activities funded under the grant after the conclusion of the grant period;

children.label: F
children.text: have submitted information pursuant to paragraph (4)(F) that demonstrates the capability to participate in rigorous evaluation of program effectiveness; and

children.label: G
children.text: are a State or public agency, or outline a plan to increase the availability of services funded under the grant statewide.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than 9 months after , the Secretary shall review indicators that are used to assess periodically the performance of the grant recipients under the Protecting America’s Children by Strengthening Families Act and establish a set of core indicators related to child safety, child permanency, reunification, re-entry into care, parental recovery, parenting capacity, and family well-being, and access to services for families with substance use disorder, including those with children who are overrepresented in foster care, difficult to place, or have disproportionately low permanency rates. In developing the core indicators, to the extent possible, indicators shall be made consistent with the outcome measures described in .September 28, 2006section 671(e)(6) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: The Assistant Secretary for the Administration for Children and Families.

children.label: ii
children.text: The Administrator of the Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration.

children.label: iii
children.text: The Administrator of the National Institute on Drug Abuse.

children.label: iv
children.text: Other stakeholders or constituencies as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Semiannual reports
children.label: i Semiannual reports
children.text: Not later than September 30 of each fiscal year in which a recipient of a grant under this subsection is paid funds under the grant, and every 6 months thereafter, the grant recipient shall submit to the Secretary a report on the services provided and activities carried out during the reporting period, progress made in achieving the goals of the program, the number of children, adults, and families receiving services, and such additional information as the Secretary determines is necessary. The report due not later than September 30 of the last such fiscal year shall include, at a minimum, data on each of the performance indicators included in the evaluation of the regional partnership.

children.heading: Incorporation of information related to performance indicators
children.label: ii Incorporation of information related to performance indicators
children.text: Each recipient of a grant under this subsection shall incorporate into the first annual report required by clause (i) that is submitted after the establishment of performance indicators under paragraph (8), information required in relation to such indicators.

children.label: i
children.text: the services provided and activities conducted with funds provided under grants awarded under this subsection;

children.label: ii
children.text: the performance indicators established under paragraph (8);

children.label: iii
children.text: the progress that has been made in addressing the needs of families with substance abuse problems who come to the attention of the child welfare system and in achieving the goals of child safety, permanence, and family stability; and

children.label: iv
children.text: whether any programs funded by the grants were submitted to the clearinghouse established under for review and the results of any such review.section 676(d) of this title

children.heading: Limitation on use of funds for administrative expenses of the Secretary
children.label: 10 Limitation on use of funds for administrative expenses of the Secretary
children.text: Not more than 5 percent of the amounts appropriated or reserved for awarding grants under this subsection may be used by the Secretary for salaries and Department of Health and Human Services administrative expenses in administering this subsection.

children.heading: Funding for the development of an electronic interstate case-processing system to expedite the interstate placement of children in foster care or guardianship, or for adoption
children.label: g Funding for the development of an electronic interstate case-processing system to expedite the interstate placement of children in foster care or guardianship, or for adoption

children.heading: Purpose
children.label: 1 Purpose
children.text: The purpose of this subsection is to facilitate the development of an electronic interstate case-processing system for the exchange of data and documents to expedite the placements of children in foster, guardianship, or adoptive homes across State lines.

children.label: i
children.text: reducing the time it takes for a child to be provided with a safe and appropriate permanent living arrangement across State lines;

children.label: ii
children.text: improving administrative processes and reducing costs in the foster care system; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the secure exchange of relevant case files and other necessary materials in real time, and timely communications and placement decisions regarding interstate placements of children.

children.label: B
children.text: A description of the activities to be funded in whole or in part with the funds, including the sequencing of the activities.

children.label: C
children.text: A description of the strategies for integrating programs and services for children who are placed across State lines.

children.label: D
children.text: Such other information as the Secretary may require.

children.heading: Funding authority
children.label: 3 Funding authority
children.text: The Secretary may provide funds to a State that complies with paragraph (2). In providing funds under this subsection, the Secretary shall prioritize States that are not yet connected with the electronic interstate case-processing system referred to in paragraph (1).

children.heading: Use of funds
children.label: 4 Use of funds
children.text: A State to which funding is provided under this subsection shall use the funding to support the State in connecting with, or enhancing or expediting services provided under, the electronic interstate case-processing system referred to in paragraph (1).

children.label: A
children.text: How using the electronic interstate case-processing system developed pursuant to paragraph (4) has changed the time it takes for children to be placed across State lines.

children.label: B
children.text: The number of cases subject to the Interstate Compact on the Placement of Children that were processed through the electronic interstate case-processing system, and the number of interstate child placement cases that were processed outside the electronic interstate case-processing system, by each State in each year.

children.label: C
children.text: The progress made by States in implementing the electronic interstate case-processing system.

children.label: D
children.text: How using the electronic interstate case-processing system has affected various metrics related to child safety and well-being, including the time it takes for children to be placed across State lines.

children.label: E
children.text: How using the electronic interstate case-processing system has affected administrative costs and caseworker time spent on placing children across State lines.

children.label: A
children.text: connecting the system with other data systems (such as systems operated by State law enforcement and judicial agencies, systems operated by the Federal Bureau of Investigation for the purposes of the Innocence Lost National Initiative, and other systems);

children.label: B
children.text: simplifying and improving reporting related to paragraphs (34) and (35) of regarding children or youth who have been identified as being a sex trafficking victim or children missing from foster care; andsection 671(a) of this title2So in original. Probably should be “being sex trafficking victims”.2

children.label: C
children.text: improving the ability of States to quickly comply with background check requirements of , including checks of child abuse and neglect registries as required by .section 671(a)(20) of this titlesection 671(a)(20)(B) of this title
